body_txt: Picture this: in a story, you have a character. They have a well-thought-out background story, a hardened goal to reach, surprising character development, and an overall influence on the story that makes them a well-established character from beginning to end. Now the next arc or sequel of the story begins, and the well-established character is there of course, fleshed out and everything. Only this time, they're not alone. Their sibling enters the picture. Wait, what? Yes, this well-established character has a sibling that shows up in the story all of a sudden, and when have we ever learned that this character has something like this? Why right now of course! The established character in question had given no indication of ever having a sibling, yet they do. This is not bad by any means. Their involvement can certainly bring more information about the established character into light, further expanding on their character development. The mere mention of a possible sibling may just never have come up because it wasn't really important. On the other hand, it might've been very important; it just wasn't important until a certain plot point in the story had either been revealed or resolved. The idea of a well long-established character having a sibling can be done right, especially if the arc focuses on them. The real issue is keeping it from feeling like so much of a shoehorn that it ruins both characters in the process. In order to qualify for this trope, the character must have known about their sibling all along. If they were previously unaware of their sibling and learned about them at the same time everyone else does, then it would have to be Long-Lost Relative and/or Separated at Birth . Sub-Trope of Unknown Relative , which involves any relative being unknown to at least one person, and Prodigal Family (estranged family members show up after a long time to complicate a character's life). Compare to Cain and Abel and Seth , Cousin Oliver , Backstory Invader , and Remember the New Guy? , for other tropes involving suddenly-introduced siblings or characters.
